title: GROUP_ADD and GROUP_LEAVE not rate-limited; each call makes 4+ DB round-trips
description: GROUP_ADD and GROUP_LEAVE have no rate-limit guard. GROUP_ADD makes: user_exists(), get_group(), is_group_member(), add_group_member_capped() — 4 DB calls per request. GROUP_LEAVE makes: get_group(), is_group_member(), remove_member_and_maybe_delete_group() — 3 DB calls per request. An authenticated user (no subscription required for GROUP_ADD/LEAVE) can issue these at the WebSocket message rate with no throttling. The rate_limit_per_min counter applies only to BROADCAST, SEALED_BROADCAST, WHISPER, SEALED_WHISPER, and GROUP_SEND. Fix: apply check_rate_limit to GROUP_ADD and GROUP_LEAVE using the same rate_limit_per_min threshold.
